overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from a Star Wars game showing a character and her creature companion exploring a desert canyon landscape on the planet Toshara, traversing sandy and rocky terrain toward and past a large natural rock arch formation.

scene_state.functional_zone: open desert wasteland with distant rock arch

events.description: character veers right off sandy path onto rocky terrain

agents.ego_description: jogging through desert sand toward a distant rock arch with a blaster rifle on her back

notable_agents.action: player character running forward through the desert
small creature companion running alongside the character

entered.detail: large natural stone arch formation in the distance
tall red rock cliff face forming the canyon wall
sandy desert floor with scattered small rocks

segments.scene_description: The character begins jogging across an open desert wasteland with a large natural rock arch visible in the distance. The terrain is sandy with sparse vegetation, flanked by tall cliff formations on the left. A small creature companion runs alongside as the character veers onto rockier ground.

scene_state.functional_zone: rocky desert terrain approaching the arch formation

agents.ego_description: running across rocky desert ground with the arch formation growing larger ahead

notable_agents.action: creature companion following closely behind the character

entered.detail: tall layered rock formation on the right side of the path
large rocks scattered across the desert floor

segments.scene_description: The character continues running across increasingly rocky terrain as the natural arch formation grows larger ahead. The landscape features scattered boulders and the canyon walls become more defined on both sides. The ground transitions from sand to gravel and rock.

scene_state.functional_zone: rocky canyon passage passing the arch landmark

events.description: character passes the rock arch formation, which moves to the left side

agents.ego_description: continuing through the rocky terrain with the arch now to the left side, moving deeper into the canyon

notable_agents.action: creature companion keeping pace with the character on the rocky ground

segments.scene_description: The character moves past the natural rock arch, which now sits to the left side of the frame while the path continues forward through the canyon. The terrain remains rocky and uneven, with the canyon walls closing in slightly. The arch gradually recedes into the background.

scene_state.functional_zone: narrowing desert canyon with high walls on both sides

events.description: entering a narrower canyon section with prominent walls flanking both sides

agents.ego_description: running through the canyon corridor with tall rock walls flanking both sides, moving toward the distant opening

notable_agents.action: companion creature running alongside on the sandy canyon floor

entered.detail: sandy and rocky ground between the canyon walls

segments.scene_description: The character enters a narrower canyon passage with high rock walls on both sides. The natural arch is no longer visible, having faded into the distance behind. The path continues forward between the canyon walls toward a distant opening, with the creature companion maintaining pace.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement across a desert wasteland that gradually narrows into a confined canyon passage. It begins with the character and a small creature companion jogging across open sandy terrain with sparse vegetation, moving toward a large natural rock arch visible in the distance. As the character veers onto rockier ground, the landscape transitions from sand to gravel and scattered boulders while the canyon walls become more defined on both sides. The scene then shifts as the character passes beneath the massive arch, which recedes into the background to the left while the path continues forward through uneven terrain. Finally, the environment closes in as the character enters a narrow passage with high rock walls, moving away from the distant arch toward a far opening.
